author: @l0I0I0I0
text:
DIY torpedoes.   Nice!    Granted I don't fully understand the concept, however was wondering if the speed/ consistency at which you break the flux line increases output power?  With that said, if you had a circular ring rod that passed through the center that was made with alternating magnetically conductive and non conductive segments though perfectly round, you could break the magnetic segments faster without having to reverse directions or slow down.  Perhaps have alternating shielding and as many breaks as you can fit on a ring.  This would also allow you to add more coils on the same round ring, doubling or tripling quadrupling the power with more coils and a larger ring, producing more power.  Now if I remember correctly, flux moves through certain dialectic materials 100,000 times better such as in the production of caps.   Yes this is another step but increasing the flux flow helps with the power formula.  
Now it needs to be lubricated.  Well NASA I think invented air lubrication by scoring the surface of the materials and placing the surfaces close enough and fast enough so air acts as self lubricating.  Yes you could skip this last step but using air as a lubricant is ideal.  Now the last step, how to turn the ring?  Well you can get creative with that last step.  The ring can have gearing build in, or can be used as a pulley of various forms and shapes, whatever.  
So in recap, instead of having a rod which stops twice, use a ring made of both alternating extreme flux conductive dialectic, and insulator to break the flux and use the same ring as a lever of sorts to flow in same direction breaking the flux lines without having to stop like a standard piston.  😅
Hope that made a lick of Sense but it's hard to draw with words.  Not 100% certain of all the operations but in the end however it's designed, redundancy of power coils and breaking flux using ring and not a rod.  🎉

author: @ryanjamesloyd6733
text:
My notion with flux switching is using a relatively small electro-magnet, wound onto 2 separated metal armatures, and a strong magnet on each armature, on N one S,   with the generating coil in between them.  The idea is that when you flip the polarity of the electromagnet, it will contain the field on One side, and reinforce the field on the other, and just wobble the two fields across the generator coil without having to move anything. 

I do not know that this would work, but I think the potential is there to use a low power generator, to wobble the field of much stronger permanent magnets across a coil, and do so very quickly, and perhaps get more bang for your generating buck.

It's just a place I Think I may see a way to do a heat-pump type exploit on the tendencies of magnetic fields.

author: @klintkrossa6885
text: This is like when people say that electricity fallows the path of least resistance. It is not true. Electricity will fallow all paths. The flux will still be there just very small.

replies.author: @overunity_inventor
replies.text:
that's true and false both, 

your home gets a phase connection and a neutral connection, 

you connect all loads of house between phase and neutral, additionally a naked thicker wire of lower resistance is connected between earth and all plugs, appliances, pipes and everything that has chances of electrical leakage,

when a human body comes in contact with an appliance which is electrically short circuited, the electricity goes to earth through the thicker earth wire and does not go to earth through the human body, because electricity takes the path of least resistance, 

but if the voltage is too high and earth wire is not thick enough to ground all the charges at once, then some of the residual charges will take the path of more resistance as well, 

so it depends on voltage.
